danish: Jeg spiser morgenmad hver morgen.
english: I eat breakfast every morning.

danish: Hun arbejder på et hospital.
english: She works at a hospital.

danish: Vi bor i en lille lejlighed i København.
english: We live in a small apartment in Copenhagen.

danish: Børnene leger i haven.
english: The children play in the garden.

danish: Bilen er rød.
english: The car is red.

danish: Huset ligger ved søen.
english: The house is by the lake.

danish: Manden læser avisen.
english: The man reads the newspaper.

danish: Kan du hjælpe mig?
english: Can you help me?

danish: Jeg gav ham en gave.
english: I gave him a gift.

danish: Vi ses i morgen.
english: See you tomorrow.

danish: I morges spiste jeg morgenmad klokken syv.
english: This morning I ate breakfast at seven o'clock.

danish: I går gik vi i biografen.
english: Yesterday we went to the cinema.

danish: I Danmark taler man dansk.
english: In Denmark people speak Danish.

danish: Desværre kan jeg ikke komme til festen.
english: Unfortunately I cannot come to the party.

danish: Normalt spiser vi aftensmad klokken seks.
english: Normally we eat dinner at six o'clock.

danish: Hver dag cykler jeg til arbejde.
english: Every day I cycle to work.

danish: Om sommeren rejser vi til Spanien.
english: In the summer we travel to Spain.

danish: Hvis det regner, tager jeg en paraply.
english: If it rains, I take an umbrella.

danish: Når jeg kommer hjem, laver jeg mad.
english: When I get home, I make food.

danish: Han spiser ikke morgenmad.
english: He does not eat breakfast.

danish: Jeg ved, at han ikke kommer i morgen.
english: I know that he is not coming tomorrow.

danish: Vi bliver hjemme, fordi det ikke regner.
english: We stay home because it is not raining.

danish: Hun kommer altid til tiden.
english: She always arrives on time.

danish: Jeg tror, at han altid spiser morgenmad.
english: I think that he always eats breakfast.

danish: Han arbejder ikke i dag, fordi han ikke er rask.
english: He is not working today because he is not well.

danish: Selvom det regnede, gik vi en tur.
english: Even though it rained, we went for a walk.

danish: Jeg lærer dansk, fordi jeg bor i Danmark.
english: I learn Danish because I live in Denmark.

danish: Da jeg var barn, boede jeg i Odense.
english: When I was a child, I lived in Odense.

danish: Jeg gik en tur, mens hun lavede mad.
english: I went for a walk while she made food.

danish: I går gik vi i biografen og så en god film.
english: Yesterday we went to the cinema and watched a good film.

danish: Jeg har boet her i tre år.
english: I have lived here for three years.

danish: Hun havde allerede spist, da jeg kom.
english: She had already eaten when I arrived.

danish: Vi skal rejse til Norge næste uge.
english: We are going to travel to Norway next week.

danish: Han drak kaffe, mens han læste avisen.
english: He drank coffee while he read the newspaper.

danish: Hvad sagde du?
english: What did you say?

danish: Spis dine grøntsager!
english: Eat your vegetables!

danish: Kom her!
english: Come here!

danish: Toget er hurtigere end bussen.
english: The train is faster than the bus.

danish: Denne film er den bedste film, jeg har set.
english: This movie is the best movie I have seen.

danish: Danmark er mindre end Sverige.
english: Denmark is smaller than Sweden.

danish: Min søster er ældre end mig.
english: My sister is older than me.

danish: Lejligheder i København er dyrere end i Odense.
english: Apartments in Copenhagen are more expensive than in Odense.

danish: Det er det største hus i byen.
english: It is the biggest house in the city.

danish: Bogen er mere interessant end filmen.
english: The book is more interesting than the movie.

danish: Han er den yngste i familien.
english: He is the youngest in the family.

danish: Peter vasker sin bil.
english: Peter washes his (own) car.

danish: Peter vasker hans bil.
english: Peter washes his (someone else's) car.

danish: Anna læser sin bog i sengen.
english: Anna reads her (own) book in bed.

danish: Min bil er rød, men hans er blå.
english: My car is red, but his is blue.

danish: Jeg gav hende mit telefonnummer.
english: I gave her my phone number.

danish: Vi hygger os derhjemme.
english: We enjoy ourselves at home.

danish: Han barberer sig hver morgen.
english: He shaves (himself) every morning.

danish: Denne bog er min, og den bog er din.
english: This book is mine, and that book is yours.

danish: Disse bøger er dyre.
english: These books are expensive.

danish: Mit hus har tre soveværelser.
english: My house has three bedrooms.

danish: Jeg kan godt lide at læse bøger.
english: I like to read books.

danish: Butikken lukker klokken seks.
english: The shop closes at six o'clock.

danish: Jeg skal til lægen i morgen.
english: I have to go to the doctor tomorrow.

danish: Dansk er et svært sprog, men det er også sjovt.
english: Danish is a difficult language, but it is also fun.

danish: Har du et spørgsmål?
english: Do you have a question?

danish: Min familie bor i Danmark.
english: My family lives in Denmark.

danish: Vejret er godt i dag, men det bliver dårligere i morgen.
english: The weather is good today, but it will get worse tomorrow.

danish: Jeg kender ikke dette ord.
english: I don't know this word.

danish: Der er mange muligheder.
english: There are many possibilities.

danish: Jeg har ikke nok penge til at købe en ny bil.
english: I don't have enough money to buy a new car.

danish: Kan du finde mine nøgler?
english: Can you find my keys?

danish: Vi drikker kaffe om morgenen og te om aftenen.
english: We drink coffee in the morning and tea in the evening.

danish: Hun skriver en mail til sin chef.
english: She writes an email to her boss.

danish: Først gik vi en tur, og bagefter spiste vi is.
english: First we went for a walk, and afterwards we ate ice cream.

danish: Inden du går, skal du rydde op.
english: Before you go, you have to clean up.

danish: Maden står på bordet.
english: The food is on the table.

danish: Han kan godt lide sit arbejde, selvom det er svært.
english: He likes his job, even though it is hard.

danish: Snart begynder sommeren.
english: Soon summer will begin.

danish: I weekenden besøger vi vores bedsteforældre.
english: On the weekend we visit our grandparents.

danish: Sidste sommer rejste vi til Italien.
english: Last summer we traveled to Italy.

danish: De snakker dansk, men de forstår ikke alt.
english: They speak Danish, but they don't understand everything.

danish: Jeg tror, at det bliver bedre med tiden.
english: I think that it will get better with time.

danish: Efter at vi havde spist, gik vi en tur.
english: After we had eaten, we went for a walk.

danish: Han sagde, at han ikke kunne komme.
english: He said that he could not come.
